about: Formal experiment log for the Twitter self-improvement loop (G6). One ACTIVE experiment at a time. The loop grades the active experiment from post_ledger.json data BEFORE starting a new one. Statuses: active | graded | abandoned.

experiments.hypothesis: Confession-flex openers (believable narrow record + self-deprecation, e.g. '16-5 and I couldn't name Austria's XI') out-earn straight record-flex cards on replies and RTs.
experiments.metric: ledger score (likes + 3*RT + 5*replies) of structure=confession-flex vs structure=record-plus-picks-card, n>=3 each

experiments.hypothesis: Posts whose close is a genuinely answerable polarizing question get >=2x the replies of posts closing with a binary tail/fade ask.
experiments.metric: avg replies of question-close vs binary-close posts in ledger, n>=4 each

experiments.hypothesis: Ref-tagged self-reply links (nhlsavant.com/?ref=tMMDDx) show bio link drives more site visits than in-post links.
experiments.metric: site analytics ref= breakdown after 2 weeks of tagging
